title: In fiscal year 2023, we paid $398 million in quarterly cash dividends. Our cash dividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividend program and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.

title: A discussion regarding our financial condition and results of operations for fiscal year 2023 compared to fiscal year 2022 is presented below. title: The estimated fair value and useful life of the acquired intangible assets at the time of the acquisition are as follows:
footnotes: (1) The fair value of developed technology was identified using the Multi-Period Excess Earnings Method.
(2) Customer relationships represent the fair value of the existing relationships using the With and Without Method.

(3) Order backlog represents primarily the fair value of purchase arrangements with customers using the Multi-Period Excess Earnings Method. The intangible asset was fully amortized as of January 31, 2021.
(4) Trade names primarily relate to Mellanox trade names and fair value was determined by applying the Relief-from-Royalty Method under the income approach.
markdown:
| Fair Value | Useful Lives |
| --- | --- |
| Developed technology (1) | $1,640 |
| Customer relationships (2) | 440 |
| Order backlog (3) | 190 |
| Trade names (4) | 70 |
| Total identified finite-lived intangible assets | 2,340 |
| IPR&D (5) | 630 |
| Total identified intangible assets | $2,970 |

(1) The fair value of developed technology was identified using the Multi-Period Excess Earnings Method.
(2) Customer relationships represent the fair value of the existing relationships using the With and Without Method.
(3) Order backlog represents primarily the fair value of purchase arrangements with customers using the Multi-Period Excess Earnings Method. The intangible asset was fully amortized as of January 31, 2021.
(4) Trade names primarily relate to Mellanox trade names and fair value was determined by applying the Relief-from-Royalty Method under the income approach.

(2) These liabilities are carried on our Consolidated Balance Sheets at their original issuance value, net of unamortized debt discount and issuance costs.
